sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: The magenta square ID=10 moved up and to the left 8 times. Let me check the current positions of all magenta squares.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']
for n in nodes:
    if n['color'] == 'M' and n['id'] in [9, 10, 11, 12]:
        print('ID=%s boundary=%s' % (n['id'], n['boundary']))
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
ID=10 is at [[34,38] to [35,39]. The blue circle is at [[23,31] to [31,37]. ID=10 needs to move up and to the left more clicks to reach the blue circle.
Wait, ID=10 is at [[34,38] to [35,39]. The blue circle is at [[23,31] to [31,37]. So ID=10 needs to move up about 3-11 rows and left about 1-10 columns to reach the blue circle.
But I'm clicking on the same coordinate repeatedly, and the magenta square is moving. Let me continue clicking on ID=10 to move it towards the blue circle.
Actually, wait - I need to update the coordinates each time since the square moves. Let me write a loop that tracks the square moves.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Continue clicking on ID=10 to move it towards the blue circle
# The square moves diagonally up-left 1 row and 1 row and left 1 col each click
# ID=10 is at [[34,38] to [35,39]
# Need to click at the top-left corner of the square
for i in range(6 in range(8):
    action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 34 - i, 'col': 38 - i}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
